reasoning: Cul2 is the scaffold protein of the CRL2 (Cullin‑2) E3 ubiquitin ligase complex, most notably the VHL‑CRL2 complex that targets the oxygen‑sensing transcription factor HIF‑α for proteasomal degradation under normoxic conditions. When Cul2 is knocked down by CRISPRi, the CRL2‑VHL complex is destabilized, leading to reduced ubiquitination and degradation of HIF‑α. Consequently, HIF‑α accumulates even in the presence of oxygen and translocates to the nucleus, where it activates transcription of hypoxia‑responsive genes. BNIP3 (Bcl‑2/adenovirus E1B 19 kDa interacting protein 3) is a well‑characterized HIF‑1 target that is induced under hypoxic conditions to promote cell death and autophagy. Therefore, loss of Cul2 is expected to increase HIF‑1 activity and consequently up‑regulate BNIP3 expression.
